description_long: This is a rustic watermill featuring a large wooden water wheel supported by a timber frame. The wheel is constructed from dark, weathered wood with visible spokes and paddles, and is mounted on a sturdy wooden axle. The supporting structure includes angled braces and a horizontal base, all made from rough-hewn timber, some of which show signs of moss and aging. The mill race, or water channel, is partially visible and appears to be made from the same wood, blending into the base of the structure. There are no visible building enclosures, roofs, or windows, indicating this is likely an exposed or open-air watermill mechanism. The overall appearance suggests a historical or rural design, suitable for placement near streams or in park settings.
description: A large, weathered wooden water wheel mounted on a timber frame with visible moss and rustic details. The structure is open, showing the wheel and supporting beams, with no enclosed building or roof.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full circular water wheel, its spokes, and the supporting timber frame with mossy details.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the wheel and the thickness of the wooden paddles and frame.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite side of the wheel and the full length of the wooden base and supports.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the wheel's edge and the angled support beams.
attribute_water_wheel: Overshot wooden water wheel, approximately 2 meters in diameter, constructed from dark, weathered timber with 8 visible spokes and multiple paddles. The wheel shows signs of aging and moss, with a simple hub and robust axle.
attribute_mill_race: Partially visible, constructed from wood, approximately 1.2 meters in visible length and 0.3 meters in width, open channel with moss and rough-hewn texture.
attribute_building_structure: No main building structure is present; only the exposed timber frame and wheel are visible.
attribute_roof_type: Not visible; no roof structure present.
